transcript: I was nervous today about whether this app can work, but I still feel it can help people keep their thoughts private.

expected.title: Concern and hope about the private app idea

transcript: I want this friend to work without the internet when someone is flying or camping.
I ate dinner at seven and watched a show.
Today was really hard. I had a fight with Sarah and I feel like I let her down. I need to apologize tomorrow.
I just had the best idea for the product. We should let users export their memories as a beautifully formatted PDF that they can print and keep.
Met with Dr. Chen about my knee. She said the MRI looks fine and I should keep doing physical therapy twice a week.
I promise myself I will read for thirty minutes every night before bed instead of scrolling my phone.

I keep coming back to this idea that privacy is the most important feature. Every time I think about the app, I think about how people need to trust it completely. Without trust, none of the other features matter.
Feeling really overwhelmed with everything going on at work. The deadline for the Q3 report is Friday and I haven't even started the analysis section.
Had the most amazing hike at Mount Tamalpais today with Alex and Jordan. The sunset from the peak was incredible. I want to remember this feeling.
I think I want to quit my job and start something on my own. I've been thinking about this for months. The corporate environment is draining me.
My daughter Maya said her first full sentence today. She said 'I want more juice please.' I almost cried. She's growing up so fast.
I noticed that every time I'm stressed about work, I end up eating junk food late at night. I want to break this pattern. Maybe I should try going for a walk instead when I feel the urge.
